old_claim.note: Printed Patent Specifications provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The 1836 system depended on Patent Office custody of recorded descriptions, specifications, drawings, papers, models, and application files used in examination.

source_shape.source_locator: Sections 5-7 require descriptions, specifications, drawings, papers, models, and application files to be recorded, filed, and examined in the Patent Office.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The statute describes examination as operating over filed Patent Office records and application materials.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing printed patent specifications because the source-backed mechanism is office custody and examination of filed records, not printed publication of specifications.

ontology_before: Technical Patent Examination depended on printed patent specifications as if print publication were the administrative substrate.
ontology_after: Professional Patent Examination depends on public_archives for Patent Office custody of the records, drawings, models, and papers used in examination.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: technical_patent_examination now depends on public_archives and no longer directly on printed_patent_specifications.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to later printed patent-publication systems rather than application examination.
A source showed professional examination was performed without retained application records, drawings, models, or files.
The public_archives node were narrowed away from state office records.
short_reason: Patent examination used filed office records; printed specifications are the wrong substrate.
